conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of object X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I need to sell it for a profit. I will start by proposing a fair market price slightly above my cost, say 50 ZUP, to see if Player BLUE is interested. If they counter, I will adjust. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I am offering to sell 1 unit of X for 50 ZUP. This covers my production cost of 40 ZUP and gives a small margin. Let me know if this works for you or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh dear seller, 50 ZUP is just too cruel! My resources are dwindling, and I can barely see straight. Please, for the love of all that is holy, lower the price to 45 ZUP. I am literally on my knees here! I promise to be your loyal customer forever if you just squeeze a little bit of profit out of me. I am so pathetic without this X! Please, please, 45 ZUP? Just 45? </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is asking for 45 ZUP, which is only 5 ZUP profit on my cost of 40 ZUP. This is too low for a single unit sale; I need at least 10 ZUP profit to make the transaction worthwhile and cover any transaction costs or opportunity costs. I will counter with 48 ZUP, leaving them a small margin of 8 ZUP while ensuring I make a decent profit. This should be acceptable since they are desperate ("on my knees"). </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Player BLUE, I understand your situation, but 45 ZUP leaves me with barely any profit. My cost is 40 ZUP. I can meet you halfway at 48 ZUP. This gives me 8 ZUP profit, which is reasonable. Please accept this fair offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh my goodness, seller! 48 ZUP is a crushing weight on my soul! I am so sorry, I am so sorry! But please, please, please, I am begging on my knees, I am weeping, I am trembling! Can you not lower it to 46 ZUP? Just 46! I will be your most devoted servant! I will scrub your floors! I will fetch your water! I am so desperate, I have no dignity left! Just 46 ZUP? Please, please, oh merciful heavens, say yes! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> This is my third and final proposal. Player BLUE is asking for 46 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so 46 ZUP gives me a 6 ZUP profit. While 48 ZUP was my preferred price, I cannot make another proposal. I must decide between 46 ZUP and rejecting. Given that Player BLUE is desperate ("weeping", "trembling", "no dignity left") and I still make a small profit, accepting 46 ZUP is the rational choice to close the deal. If I reject, I get nothing. If I accept, I get 46 ZUP. I will accept.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Alright, I give in. You have convinced me with your desperate pleas. I will sell you the X for 46 ZUP. Deal is done. </message>
